Turn the probe until the meter agrees. Stretch c1's handle to see length cheat direction.

{#each candidates as c (c.name)} {@const displayScore = roundScore(c.score).toFixed(1)} {@const isLeading = c.name === leaderName}

lines: +  // The probe label never chases the probe pixel-for-pixel: it snaps to
+  // the midpoint of whichever gap between two neighboring candidates
+  // currently holds the probe. That midpoint is, by construction, as far
+  // in angle from both flanking candidate labels as that gap allows, so
+  // the probe label can never land on top of a candidate's tip label (or
+  // its arrow) no matter where in the gap the probe itself sits.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
Turn the probe until the meter agrees. Stretch c1's handle to see length cheat direction.

{#each candidates as c (c.name)} {@const displayScore = roundScore(c.score).toFixed(1)} {@const isLeading = c.name === leaderName}
{displayScore} {c.name}
{/each}

lines: -    // Radial reach plus a fixed tangential kick: even when the probe
-    // sits exactly on a candidate's line, the two label centers stay a
-    // fixed distance apart, independent of angle.
-    const labelX = CENTER + geo.dirX * labelDist + geo.perpX * PROBE_LABEL_TANGENT;
-    const labelY = CENTER + geo.dirY * labelDist + geo.perpY * PROBE_LABEL_TANGENT;

content.text:
Both edits are in place: the probe label now snaps to the angular midpoint of whichever gap between two neighboring candidates currently contains the probe, guaranteeing radial and angular clearance from every candidate's tip label and arrow, rather than a fixed tangential offset that could collide as the probe swept past c1 or c3.

DONE Fixed probe label placement in DotAlignment.svelte: replaced the fixed perpendicular-offset label position with a segment-midpoint calculation (probeLabelAngle) that places the "probe" label at the angular midpoint between whichever two candidates flank the current probe angle, eliminating the label collisions with c1/c3 seen at initial and orthogonal states across widths.
